title: Aquarium
name: Aquarium
intro: Aquarium is a water-filling logic puzzle played on a grid divided into irregular regions called aquariums or tanks.
goal: Your goal is to fill cells with water to match the numbers along the edges.
board_example: Below is an empty Aquarium puzzle. Numbers show how many cells in each row/column should contain water:
rule_hints: Row & Column hints: The numbers along the top and left edges indicate how many cells in that row or column must be filled with water.
rule_gravity: Water obeys gravity: Within each aquarium, if a cell contains water, all cells below it in the same column within that aquarium must also contain water.
rule_independent: Aquariums are independent: Different aquariums can have different water levels - water doesn't flow between tanks.
solved: A puzzle is solved when all row and column counts are satisfied and water obeys gravity within each aquarium.
control_cycle: Click a cell to cycle through states:

compact.rule_hints: Numbers show how many water cells per row/column
compact.rule_gravity: Water fills from bottom up within each aquarium
compact.rule_independent: Different aquariums have independent water levels
